task_context.description: This is a comprehensive reverse-engineering and simulation validation task that involves extracting design specifications from a technical document, implementing a computational physics simulation based on those specifications, and then using the simulation results to propose design improvements. The task specifically requires autonomous document analysis to extract detector geometry and material properties, followed by GDML-based Geant4 simulation implementation, particle shower physics evaluation, and iterative design optimization based on simulation outcomes rather than literature comparisons.
task_context.goal: Extract CMS ECAL design parameters from technical documentation, implement a simplified Geant4 simulation of the electromagnetic calorimeter, evaluate electron shower performance characteristics, and propose design improvements based on simulation results
constraints: Extract DESIGN specifications only, not measured performance values
Use simplified geometry (single module or tower for tractable simulation)
Use homogeneous PbWO4 block or projective tower geometry
Do NOT compare simulation results against measured values from the paper
Must test at least one alternative configuration for improvements

observations.content: Step completed successfully: Extract CMS ECAL design parameters from technical documents including PbWO4 crystal dimensions, material properties, and geometry specifications
Step completed successfully: Generate GDML geometry for baseline CMS ECAL design using PbWO4 crystals in homogeneous slab configuration
Step completed successfully: Simulate electron showers in baseline CMS ECAL geometry across energy range to establish performance baseline
Step completed successfully: Analyze simulation results for baseline CMS configuration: energy resolution, shower containment, and transverse spread
Step completed successfully: Generate alternative GDML geometry with optimized crystal dimensions for improved energy resolution
Step completed successfully: Simulate electron showers in improved geometry configuration to evaluate design modifications
Step completed successfully: Analyze simulation results for improved design configuration: energy resolution, shower containment, and transverse spread
Step completed successfully: Compare performance metrics between baseline CMS and improved design configurations across all energies
Step completed successfully: Generate comprehensive report comparing baseline vs improved CMS ECAL design with performance metrics and optimization recommendations
